title: RAF Operational Low Flying Training Timetable Released

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content:
The Royal Air Force (RAF) has released its operational low flying training timetable, detailing schedules for training flights across three areas in the UK. This guidance is essential for those residing near designated low flying routes to remain informed and aware of scheduled activities.

The low flying operations are part of the RAF's training procedures, ensuring pilots gain the necessary experience in low altitude flying. The RAF plays a crucial role in the UK's aerial defense strategy, with these training exercises contributing to national and regional security. Aviation has been a significant aspect of Scotland's transport network, with HIAL airports connecting communities across the Highlands and Islands, where aviation has played a vital role since the inter-war period.
